result:
Subject: Yesterday's payments outage — what happened, and what we're doing about it

Hi there,

Yesterday, our payments service was degraded for approximately four hours. During that window, some of you saw transactions fail at checkout — payments that should have gone through didn't, and we know that's more than an inconvenience. Whether it cost you a sale, a customer's trust, or just your time, we're sorry. You rely on us to move money, and yesterday we didn't hold up our end.

The root cause was a misconfigured cache invalidation rule that behaved correctly under normal load but broke down under a traffic spike, causing stale data to be served to the payment path and transactions to fail. Once we identified it, we corrected the rule and restored full service. The fix is already in production and has been stable since.

To make sure this doesn't happen again, we're rolling out two changes: all cache and configuration changes will now go through canary rollouts so a bad rule can only affect a small slice of traffic before being caught, and we're adding chaos testing in staging to specifically exercise traffic-spike and cache-invalidation failure modes before they can reach production. These aren't one-off patches — they're permanent additions to how we ship.

If your transactions were affected, we'd like to offer a goodwill credit as a small acknowledgment that the cost of this fell on you, not just on us. You'll receive a separate email with the details in the next 24 hours; if you believe you were impacted and don't hear from us, please reply to this message and we'll make it right. Thank you for your patience, and for continuing to trust us with your payments.

— The Payments Team
